title: Boat Hire in Santorini: A Complete Guide to Costs and Seasons for 2026
html:
When you want to explore the caldera, a boat rental Santorini offers a unique perspective on this volcanic island. Departing from Vlychada Marina or Athinios Port, expect to pay from €250 for a half-day motorboat to €1,200 for a crewed yacht. BoatHire24 connects you with verified listings for a seamless experience.

Where to set off in Santorini

Your departure point shapes the voyage. Most charter boats operate from Vlychada Marina, a sheltered harbour on the south coast with easy access to the caldera and southern anchorages. Athinios Port is the main ferry port but also hosts a few bareboat operators. For a more relaxed start, consider Ammoudi Bay at the foot of Oia, though it is limited to small boats. The old port of Fira, accessible by cable car, is a hub for day trips and catamaran tours. Nearby, the island of Nea Kameni is a popular destination for volcano hikes, just 4 nautical miles from Fira. To the south, the Red Beach and White Beach offer secluded coves reachable only by water.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Santorini?

Prices start around €100 per person for a shared tour and exceed €3,000 for a luxury crewed yacht. For a small motorboat for half a day, budget from €250 to €450. A catamaran full-day trip for up to 12 guests ranges from €800 to €1,500. Crewed yachts, such as a 50-foot Lagoon, cost from €2,000 for a full day including skipper and fuel. The price often includes fuel, insurance, and basic safety equipment, but check hire companies for extras like water toys or meals. Use the boat rental Santorini page to compare verified boat listings and find the best deal for your group.

Which boat to rent

Choose based on your group size and experience. Here are popular options:

  1. Small motorboat (4-6 guests) ideal for couples or small families exploring the caldera independently, from €250 per half-day.
  2. Catamaran (up to 12 guests) perfect for a stable, spacious day sailing to the hot springs and Thirassia, from €800 per day.
  3. Speedboat (8-10 guests) with a powerful engine for quick island hopping, e.g. the Olympic 490, from €600 per half-day. View the Mefasa 90 listing for a luxury option with a flybridge and sun loungers.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (12+ guests) for a premium experience with skipper, host, and onboard jet ski, from €2,500 per day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Santorini?

Rental companies require a valid boat licence for bareboat charters, but skippered boats and small motorboats often need no licence. For Greece, you typically need an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent national licence for powerboats over a certain power or size. However, many hire operators provide a small boat (e.g. up to 30hp) without a licence, accompanied by a safety briefing. Always confirm with the charter company before booking.

For anchorages, aim to moor at the Red Beach by 10am to secure a spot, as later arrivals often find the bay crowded even in shoulder season.

The high season in Santorini runs from June to September, when the strong Meltemi wind can make the sea choppy, particularly in the afternoon. Spring (April-May) and autumn (October) offer calmer seas, fewer tourists, and lower prices.

At a glance

  • Price range: €100, €3,000 per person or charter
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September 2026
  • Trip length: Half-day (4h) to full-day (8h)
  • Capacity: 4 to 12 guests typically
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Santorini

Can I rent a boat in Santorini without a captain?

Yes, you can rent a small motorboat without a skipper if you have the required licence, but most catamarans and yachts come with a crew for safety and convenience.

What is the best boat for a sunset cruise in Santorini?

A catamaran or a luxury motor yacht like the Azimut 55 offers excellent onboard comfort and panoramic views for watching the sun set from the caldera.

Are meals included in boat hire prices?

Many shared tours and crewed charters include lunch or snacks, while bareboat rentals require you to bring your own provisions.
